summary: Justin demonstrates how to submit deals to lender partners through the AIOS portal backend system. He reviews three lending partners (Walker Dunlap, MVP Capital, Groves Capital) and their specializations—Freddie/Fannie Mae direct conduit lending, CMBS/renovation loans, and creative nationwide capital sourcing respectively. This resource streamlines lender access but doesn't directly address Brian's stated pain points of deal sourcing or underwriting.
takeaways: Walker Dunlap specializes in Freddie/Fannie Mae direct conduit with typical $1 million+ loan amounts and non-recourse debt terms
Walker Dunlap can broker CMBS loans with local commercial banks and provide renovation components alongside acquisition loans
MVP Capital targets CMBS and local commercial bank loans specifically for value-add deals with medium-to-heavy construction components
Groves Capital operates nationwide and handles 'any types of capital, fix and flips, apartment deals, any types of commercial real estate, any types of loans' with creative structuring
All three lenders operate nationwide and can handle 99.999% of commercial real estate loan types
Portal submission process includes direct application forms (e.g., Groves Capital link) plus direct lender contact information for email outreach

skill_candidates.change: Know which lender to approach based on loan type (Freddie/Fannie vs CMBS vs creative nationwide capital)
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin distinguishes Walker Dunlap for GSE lending, MVP for CMBS/renovation, and Groves for creative/nationwide deals, enabling faster lender matching

title: SUBMIT DEAL TO LENDERS
